title: vicia costata subsp. ciceroidea
intro: Vicia costata subsp. ciceroidea, also known as Cicero's vetch, is a subspecies of the perennial legume plant Vicia costata, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Cicero's vetch include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to act as an antiseptic and antispasmodic. In herbal preparations, Cicero's vetch is typically used as a dried herb or root, and can be consumed as a tea, capsule, or tincture.
